weather.summary: Saturday: 9°C, light rain. Sunday: 10°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with fun activities for your little one! From free museum visits with hands-on exhibits at the Young V&A and the Science Museum, to exciting workshops at the Southbank Centre and vibrant community events at the Horniman Museum, there's something for everyone. Don't forget to check out the free Easter Egg Hunt in Aldgate starting this week!

activities.title: Leaders in Community X Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: A special event at Young V&A, designed for community engagement and creative exploration.
activities.whyItWorks: Young V&A is designed with children in mind, offering hands-on activities and exhibits suitable for all ages. The event provides a stimulating environment with space to move and engage.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station to the museum.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific event details and timings.

activities.title: Make-Lab Masterclass: Wind Machines
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: A workshop at Young V&A where children can learn and build their own wind machines.
activities.whyItWorks: This masterclass offers a hands-on creative activity that sparks imagination and teaches basic engineering concepts. It's perfect for curious minds and provides a fun learning experience.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station to the museum.
transport.tip: Check for age suitability and book in advance as spaces may be limited.

activities.title: Play Saturdays
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Drop-in play sessions at Young V&A, offering a variety of activities for children of all ages.
activities.whyItWorks: These drop-in sessions provide a flexible and engaging play environment. With different activities on offer, there's something to capture every child's interest and energy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station to the museum.
transport.tip: Arrive early to make the most of the activities, as it can get busy.

activities.title: SEND Play
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Drop-in sessions at Young V&A specifically designed for children with Special Educational Needs and Disabilities.
activities.whyItWorks: These sessions offer a supportive and inclusive play environment tailored to the needs of children with sensory sensitivities. The museum provides a safe and welcoming space for all children to explore and enjoy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green. It's a short walk from the station to the museum.
transport.tip: Check the Young V&A website for specific session times and details.

activities.title: Easter Egg Hunt
activities.description: A free egg hunt across Aldgate, where participants follow clues hidden in business windows to win treats.
activities.whyItWorks: This outdoor activity encourages exploration and problem-solving. It provides a fun and engaging way to discover the Aldgate area while enjoying a sweet reward.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Hammersmith & City or Metropolitan Line to Aldgate East, then walk to Aldgate Square.
transport.tip: Start at Aldgate Tap to begin the trail and collect your chocolate reward upon completion.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts at Vauxhall City Farm, where you can also see goats, sheep and smaller animals.
activities.whyItWorks: The opportunity to see and interact with farm animals is great for young children. The bonnet-making craft provides a hands-on creative activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria line to Vauxhall
transport.tip: Check the Vauxhall City Farm website for specific session times and details.

activities.title: Cinema Chill: Peace at Last
activities.description: A special screening for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: A perfect introduction to cinema for toddlers, with an age-appropriate film.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to Whitechapel station then a short walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Check the Half Moon Theatre website for specific session times and details.

activities.title: Family Fun at the Young V&A Museum
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Explore the newly transformed Young V&A with its three permanent gallery spaces: Imagine, Play and Design. Get hands-on with interactive displays, workshops, and an Open Studio.
activities.whyItWorks: The Young V&A is co-designed with children and young people, ensuring the exhibits are engaging and relevant. The museum offers a mix of interactive displays and creative activities, catering to different interests and skill levels.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station. The museum is a short walk from there.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for any temporary exhibitions or special events that may require separate tickets.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for giant Easter eggs hidden around the park and learning about wildlife.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines the excitement of an egg hunt with the educational and entertaining experience of visiting the zoo. Seeing the animals is always a hit!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Highbury & Islington, then the Victoria Line to Euston, then the Northern Line to Camden Town, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time and money.

activities.title: Spring Fair
activities.address: 100 London Rd, Forest Hill, London SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Enjoy music, circus skill workshops and a 'Smallympics'.
activities.whyItWorks: The Horniman offers a diverse range of activities suitable for a 3-year-old, from exploring the gardens to participating in interactive workshops. The Spring Fair provides a festive and engaging atmosphere with plenty of opportunities for hands-on fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill station. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Explore the museum's gardens and animal enclosure for a full day of fun.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Explore the palace grounds looking for Lindt Gold Bunny statues and collect a chocolate treat upon completion.
activities.whyItWorks: The Easter trail motivates kids to walk around and explore the beautiful palace grounds. The chocolate treat at the end is a great reward!

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Liverpool Street, then a train to Hampton Court. The palace is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Go early in the day to avoid crowds and make the most of your visit.

activities.title: Royal Mews Interactive Storytelling
activities.description: Visit Buckingham Palace for interactive storytelling and crafts in the Royal Mews
activities.whyItWorks: Provides an interactive experience with stories and crafts at an iconic London landmark

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria line to Green Park
transport.tip: Check the Royal Collection Trust website for specific session times and details.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits and discover how the world works. See iconic objects from Apollo 10 to the first Apple computer.
activities.whyItWorks: The Science Museum offers a hands-on learning experience that sparks curiosity and encourages exploration. With exhibits catering to different age groups, there's something for every child to enjoy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington station. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for any special events or temporary exhibitions.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Rd, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Explore the natural world and discover fascinating creatures. Visit the dinosaur exhibit and learn about evolution.
activities.whyItWorks: The Natural History Museum offers a captivating and educational experience for children of all ages. The dinosaur exhibit is a guaranteed hit, and the museum's interactive displays make learning fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington station. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and make the most of your visit.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.address: 100 London Road, Forest Hill, London, SE23 3PQ
activities.description: Explore extensive collections of anthropology, natural history and musical instruments and enjoy the gardens, animal enclosure and aquarium.
activities.whyItWorks: The Horniman offers a diverse range of activities suitable for a 3-year-old, from exploring the gardens to seeing the animals and exploring the museum's collections. The museum provides a stimulating and engaging environment for children of all ages.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill station. The museum is a short walk from the station.
transport.tip: Check the museum's website for any special events or temporary exhibitions.

activities.title: Southbank Centre Spring Family Fun
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families at the Southbank Centre, including shows, musical adventures, puppetry, and free activities.
activities.whyItWorks: The Southbank Centre offers a diverse range of activities suitable for a 3-year-old, from music and theatre to hands-on workshops. The mix of free and paid events allows for flexibility and caters to different budgets.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street, then the tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check the Southbank Centre website for a detailed schedule of events and ticket information.

activities.title: Gozzle Nature Trail
activities.address: Queen Elizabeth's Walk, Barnes, London SW13 9WT
activities.description: Follow an interactive outdoor trail charting Gozzle's journey, inspired by the picture book from Julia Donaldson and Sara Ogilvie.
activities.whyItWorks: This outdoor trail encourages exploration and learning about nature in an engaging way. The interactive elements and connection to a popular children's book make it especially appealing to young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then the Jubilee Line to Waterloo, then a train to Barnes Bridge, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Check the London Wetland Centre website for specific dates and times, and book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Kew Gardens - Bluey at Kew
activities.description: Bluey-themed interactive activities for the whole family, including a Play Zone with 3D installations and the chance to meet the Bluey character.
activities.whyItWorks: Kew Gardens offers a beautiful outdoor setting for a fun family day out. The Bluey theme will be especially appealing to young children, and the interactive activities provide opportunities for play and learning.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then the Jubilee Line to Waterloo, then a train to Kew Bridge, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time and money.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Attend a performance of Alice in Wonderland at Riverside Studios in Hammersmith.
activities.whyItWorks: The show is designed for families and offers an engaging theatrical experience for children. The vibrant characters and imaginative storyline will capture their attention and spark their imagination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Hammersmith & City Line to Hammersmith station, then walk to Riverside Studios.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to secure your seats and check the show's age suitability.

activities.title: Your Toys
activities.description: A heart-warming show aimed at 5-9s.
activities.whyItWorks: Great theatre for young children

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus to London Bridge station then a short walk to the theatre.
transport.tip: Check the Unicorn Theatre website for specific session times and details.

activities.title: Octonauts: Above and Beyond
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens. An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines an interactive trail with the setting of Kew Gardens

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then the Jubilee Line to Waterloo, then a train to Kew Bridge, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance to save time and money.

activities.title: Hamleys' Gootopia Experience Zone
activities.description: An immersive experience at London's biggest toy shop.
activities.whyItWorks: Hamleys offers a fun, interactive experience for children

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central line to Oxford Circus
transport.tip: Check the Hamleys website for specific session times and details.

activities.title: The Magic of India Holi Colour Dance Festival
activities.description: Enjoy dance performances including bangra and dhol troops, DJs, a dancing colour-play area with safe (washable) powder colours, Indian food stalls and more.
activities.whyItWorks: The Holi Colour Dance Festival provides an interactive experience with music, dance and colours. This event would appeal to a 3 year old because of the music and colours.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Jubilee line to Swiss Cottage
transport.tip: The dancing colour-play area involves washable powder colours, so dress accordingly.
